skill.name: typescript-strict-guard
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through strict TypeScript patterns, explicit typing, type guards, React typing, and a read-only validator.
skill.summary: Enforce strict TypeScript patterns with guides, examples, and a read-only validator.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but sampled evidence shows the hits are mainly Markdown TypeScript examples, official links, and a read-only validator. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, command execution, or malicious network behavior was confirmed. Publish with a low-risk note because examples mention environment secrets and browser storage without full security caveats.

low_findings.title: Static command execution hits are Markdown false positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The Ruby or shell backtick detections align with Markdown code fences and inline TypeScript notation, not executable shell commands. The skill frontmatter only allows Read and Grep.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled locations are Markdown fences or inline formatting. No subprocess, shell execution, eval, or similar runtime command execution was found in validate-types.py.

low_findings.title: Network detections are documentation examples and reference links
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Fetch calls and URLs appear in instructional TypeScript examples or official documentation references. No executable skill code performs network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative network hits are inside Markdown examples or documentation lists. The executable Python validator imports no network libraries and performs no outbound calls.

low_findings.title: Credential and storage examples need user judgment
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW_RISK. The guides include examples typing process.env secrets and a localStorage hook. They are not exfiltration, but copied examples could be unsafe if used for sensitive browser data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly mention DATABASE_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY, and browser localStorage. The context is educational typing guidance, so the security impact is low but real if copied without review.

low_findings.title: Filesystem access is read-only validation behavior
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The validator reads user-selected TypeScript files and recursively validates a user-selected directory. It does not write files, delete files, execute code, or access hidden configuration by default.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The executable code uses pathlib to read provided files and directories only. No write, delete, subprocess, or network behavior was found in the sampled script.

content.value_statement: Strict TypeScript errors slow delivery and hide runtime defects. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete patterns for safer typing.
actual_capabilities: Explains fixes for common strict mode violations, including any, ts-ignore, non-null assertions, and implicit any.
Provides reusable type guard patterns for primitives, objects, arrays, branded types, and JSON validation.
Covers React TypeScript patterns for props, events, hooks, refs, context, forms, and server components.
Guides third-party library typing through declarations, module augmentation, global types, and tsconfig patterns.
Includes a Python validator that reads TypeScript files and reports selected strict typing violations.
Documents test expectations for type guards, utility types, async flows, React components, and error handling.
limitations: The validator uses regex checks and cannot replace the TypeScript compiler or ESLint.
Many examples are documentation snippets and may need adaptation before production use.
The guides reference TypeScript 5.6 and may not cover newer language changes.
Security guidance is limited to typing patterns and does not perform dependency or runtime vulnerability analysis.

use_cases.title: Review strict mode failures
use_cases.description: Identify safer replacements for any, ts-ignore, missing return types, and non-null assertions before merging a pull request.

use_cases.title: Build typed React components
use_cases.description: Apply typed props, event handlers, hooks, refs, context, and form patterns while building React interfaces.

use_cases.title: Integrate untyped packages
use_cases.description: Create declarations, module augmentations, wrapper types, and tests for libraries with incomplete TypeScript support.

prompt_templates.title: Fix one strict error
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small TypeScript snippet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review this TypeScript snippet. Explain each strict mode issue and rewrite it without any, ts-ignore, or non-null assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Type a React component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the React TypeScript patterns to design typed props, event handlers, state, and refs for this component. Include concise reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Create type guards
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the type guard library patterns to validate this unknown API payload. Include guard functions, error handling, and test cases.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a TypeScript module
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced code review across a module or pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill as a strict TypeScript reviewer. Inspect the module for unsafe typing, missing validation, weak assertions, and incomplete tests.

output_examples.input: A function returns data from an API as any and uses ts-ignore to bypass an error.
output_examples.output: The response should be treated as unknown, validated with a type guard, and returned as a typed result with explicit error handling.

output_examples.input: A React form stores state without typed events or validation errors.
output_examples.output: The result includes typed form data, React event types, a structured error object, and tests for valid and invalid input.

output_examples.input: A package has no published TypeScript declarations.
output_examples.output: The result recommends a declaration file, typed wrapper functions, module augmentation when needed, and compile-time type tests.

best_practices: Run the TypeScript compiler and project tests after applying any suggested typing change.
Validate external data at runtime before narrowing unknown values into application types.
Use the smallest accurate type and document exceptions when strict typing cannot be completed immediately.
anti_patterns: Do not use any, ts-ignore, or non-null assertions to silence errors without a documented reason.
Do not copy documentation examples into production without reviewing security and runtime behavior.
Do not rely on the included validator as the only strictness gate for a TypeScript project.

faq.question: Does this skill compile TypeScript code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and a simple validator, but projects still need tsc, linting, and tests.

faq.question: Can it help remove any from existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guides show replacements using unknown, explicit interfaces, generics, and runtime type guards.

faq.question: Does it support React TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers props, events, hooks, refs, context, forms, composition, and related test patterns.

faq.question: Is the included validator safe to run?
faq.answer: It reads user-selected TypeScript files and prints findings. It does not execute code, write files, or make network requests.

faq.question: Can it type third-party libraries?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains declaration files, module augmentation, wrappers, global types, and compile-time type tests.

faq.question: Does it replace security review?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on typing quality. Security review, dependency scanning, and runtime validation remain separate responsibilities.
